title: SystemVerilog: Function call by named association not supported
body:
SystemVerilog allows the usage of function calls with named association to pass arguments. Icarus generates an invalid syntax error when this is used.
I'm using iverilog 10.1 (stable).

Example code:
```systemverilog
package TestPkg;

    function automatic logic [31:0] encode(
        logic [11:0] a,
        logic [4:0] b,
        logic [2:0] c,
        logic [4:0] d,
        logic [6:0] e
    );
        begin
            encode[31:20] = a;
            encode[19:15] = b;
            encode[14:12] = c;
            encode[11:7] = d;
            encode[6:0] = e;
        end
    endfunction

endpackage
```
Module that works, compiled with `iverilog -Wall -tnull -g2012 TestPkg.sv FuncUnnamedAssoc.sv` :
```systemverilog
module FuncUnnamedAssoc(
    output logic [31:0] encoded,
    input logic [11:0] f,
    input logic [4:0] g,
    input logic [2:0] h,
    input logic [4:0] i,
    input logic [6:0] j
);

import TestPkg::*;

always @(*) begin
    encoded = encode(
        f,
        g,
        h,
        i,
        j
    );
end
```
Module that generates a syntax error, compiled with `iverilog -Wall -tnull -g2012 TestPkg.sv FuncNamedAssoc.sv` :
```systemverilog
module FuncNamedAssoc(
    output logic [31:0] encoded,
    input logic [11:0] f,
    input logic [4:0] g,
    input logic [2:0] h,
    input logic [4:0] i,
    input logic [6:0] j
);

import TestPkg::*;

always @(*) begin
    encoded = encode(
        .a(f),
        .b(g),
        .c(h),
        .d(i),
        .e(j)
    );
end

endmodule
```
Error message is:
FuncNamedAssoc.sv:14: syntax error
FuncNamedAssoc.sv:19: error: malformed statement
code_blocks:
module FuncUnnamedAssoc(
    output logic [31:0] encoded,
    input logic [11:0] f,
    input logic [4:0] g,
    input logic [2:0] h,
    input logic [4:0] i,
    input logic [6:0] j
);

import TestPkg::*;

always @(*) begin
    encoded = encode(
        f,
        g,
        h,
        i,
        j
    );
end

module FuncNamedAssoc(
    output logic [31:0] encoded,
    input logic [11:0] f,
    input logic [4:0] g,
    input logic [2:0] h,
    input logic [4:0] i,
    input logic [6:0] j
);

import TestPkg::*;

always @(*) begin
    encoded = encode(
        .a(f),
        .b(g),
        .c(h),
        .d(i),
        .e(j)
    );
end

endmodule
